{"step_by_step_thinking": "Hyaline membrane disease, also known as Respiratory Distress Syndrome (RDS), is a condition primarily affecting preterm infants due to surfactant deficiency. Surfactant deficiency leads to increased surface tension in the alveoli, causing alveolar collapse and impaired gas exchange. The key physiological consequence is reduced lung compliance and decreased functional residual capacity (FRC). According to the retrieved documents, neonates, especially preterm infants, have a reduced FRC and a higher closing capacity.
